# Bitcoin Merchant Community

> This is the agent-friendly navigation document (llms.txt) for the Bitcoin Merchant Community website. It provides a structured overview of all public content available at bitcoinmerchants.org.

The Bitcoin Merchant Community (BMC) is a growing global community of small businesses helping one another defeat 3% credit card fees by accepting bitcoin. It is supported by Block's Spiral division, which funds open-source bitcoin tools and promotes bitcoin adoption globally.

## Main Website

- [Home](https://bitcoinmerchants.org/): The Bitcoin Merchant Community — helping small businesses keep more of their profit by accepting bitcoin payments instead of credit cards.
- [Apply to Become a Local Champion](https://bitcoinmerchants.org/apply): Application page for bitcoiners who want to become Local Champions — community leaders who distribute onboarding materials and plushies to merchants and fellow bitcoiners in their area.

## Sections

### Hero
The BMC helps small businesses keep more of their profit. The Bitcoin Merchant Community is a growing global community of small businesses helping one another defeat 3% credit card fees by accepting bitcoin.

### Connect with the Community
Thousands of merchants in the US and around the world already accept bitcoin payments. The BMC Facebook Group lets merchants learn from one another's experiences. BTC Map (btcmap.org) is a collaborative, open-source directory that shows bitcoin-friendly merchants on a map.

- [BMC Facebook Group](https://www.facebook.com/groups/bitcoinmerchants/): Community group for merchants and bitcoiners to share experiences and tips.
- [BTC Map](https://btcmap.org/map): Find and list bitcoin-accepting merchants near you.

### How to Accept Bitcoin (Square)
If you already use Square, you can start accepting bitcoin today with just a few taps. Steps:
- On Square POS device: Settings > Checkout > Payments > Activate Bitcoin (requires POS software 6.81+).
- On web: Dashboard > Banking > Bitcoin > Get Started.
- Choose to settle payments in bitcoin or auto-convert to dollars (0% fee until 2027, then 1%).
- Optionally appear on a map of bitcoin-accepting merchants.
- Get a free Bitcoin Marketing Kit from Square Shop.

### Alternative Payment Solutions (non-Square / non-US)
For merchants not using Square or outside the US, the BMC recommends:

- [IBEX Pay](https://www.ibexpay.io/): A web-based bitcoin point-of-sale solution that works on any device with a browser.
- [BTCPay Server](https://btcpayserver.org/): A highly customizable, open-source self-hosted POS solution for tech-savvy merchants.
- [Manna](https://mannabitcoin.com/): A simple mobile wallet with a built-in POS interface; no personal information required to start.
- [Blink](https://www.blink.sv/): A reliable mobile wallet with a simple web-based POS interface and an optional stable-dollar account.

Contact bitcoinmerchants@spiral.xyz to suggest additional POS solutions.

### Onboard Merchants (for Bitcoiners)
Bitcoiners can download a free ready-to-print Merchant Onboarding Kit containing a 1-page leave-behind and a guide to confidently onboard merchants.

- [Download Merchant Onboarding Kit](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bitcoinmerchants/marketing-assets/main/Merchant%20Onboarding%20Kit.pdf): Free PDF kit for helping local businesses accept bitcoin.
- [BMC Bitcoiner Discord](https://discord.gg/f2xHv5QYK7): Community Discord for bitcoiners advocating for bitcoin merchant adoption.

### Find a Local Champion
Local Champions are community leaders who have free Itty Bitty plushies and leave-behind materials to help merchants get started. Use the interactive map on the BMC website to find a Local Champion near you.

### Become a Local Champion
The BMC is looking for natural leaders with deep connections to their local community. Accepted champions receive a free box of Itty Bitty plushies and leave-behind materials (25 plushies; box is 26x18x17 inches, 12 lbs). Requirements: active in your local bitcoin community, well-connected locally, able to store assets, and willing to share feedback in the BMC Discord.

- [Apply to Become a Local Champion](https://bitcoinmerchants.org/apply): Application form for prospective Local Champions worldwide (US participants may be prioritized due to shipping).

## Frequently Asked Questions

- **What is bitcoin?** Bitcoin is a secure digital currency that lets you send, spend, and save money without relying on banks or other third parties — just like cash.
- **What are bitcoin payments?** Accept bitcoin by offering customers a QR code invoice at checkout that they scan to pay from their bitcoin wallet instantly.
- **What are the fees?** With Square: 0% until 2027, then 1%. Other systems generally range from 0.4% to 1% — always less than credit card fees.
- **What about bitcoin's volatility?** Enable auto-conversions on Square to capture the merchant benefit (no 3% fees) without exposure to short-term price volatility.
- **How are bitcoin payments taxed?** Consult a tax advisor. Square merchants have tax documentation automatically generated in their dashboard. Capital gains taxes may apply if you hold bitcoin.
- **How is tipping handled?** The tip amount is defined by the customer before the QR code invoice is generated; some POS systems segregate tips by account, others aggregate them.
- **Why would customers pay in bitcoin?** Bitcoin spenders view it as the fairest way to pay. Offering discounts for bitcoin purchases can attract additional customers.
- **More questions?** Ask in the [BMC Facebook Group](https://www.facebook.com/groups/bitcoinmerchants/).

## Links & Resources

- [GitHub](https://github.com/bitcoinmerchants)
- [Facebook Group](https://www.facebook.com/groups/bitcoinmerchants/)
- [Discord](https://discord.gg/f2xHv5QYK7)
- [BTC Map](https://btcmap.org)
- [Square Bitcoin Setup Guide](https://squareup.com/us/en/bitcoin)
- [Merchant Onboarding Kit PDF](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bitcoinmerchants/marketing-assets/main/Merchant%20Onboarding%20Kit.pdf)
- Contact: bitcoinmerchants@spiral.xyz
